mnemonics: Every Location Is Numbered (ELIN) for quick access.
Addresses are like a PO Box number; the data is the mail inside.
Memory is a massive hotel (MAR is the room number you ask for, MDR is the suitcase you bring to/from that room).
keyPoints: An address is a unique number that identifies a specific location in memory.
The Memory Address Register (MAR) holds the address of the data to be accessed.
The Memory Data Register (MDR) holds the data that is read from or written to the address in the MAR.
commonMistakes: Confusing the memory address (location) with the memory data (content stored at that location).
Thinking the Memory Data Register (MDR) is part of the main memory itself, rather than a CPU register for temporary data holding during transfer.
